text: Ka wʊ pãa de a paanʊ̃ɔsɩ ayopõi nɩ a jãbãlãa ala na wʊ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan, tɩ na wʊ ŋma de kʊ a wʊ poturbo, ka ba de põ a nɩbɛ.
translated: He went to the market early in the morning to buy jãbãlãa, then came to Puor Nãaŋmɩn's place so that he could see it, because he wanted to do it properly.

ref: He took her by the hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served Jesus a meal.
text: Wʊ sɩɩr a pɔɔ nũu, ka a ãgãn tʊl baar a wʊ ir maal bʊ̃diir kʊ wʊ.
translated: He sleeps and goes home, because he eats much bread in his house and stays there.

ref: When the man saw Jesus in the distance, he ran up to him and knelt down.
text: Wʊn wa nyɛ a Yesu na be tɛɛr, wʊ jo naa tɩ lo gbur dumo a wʊ niem,
translated: When Jesus was there, he came and spoke a great message to them.

ref: That evening the owner of the vineyard told the man in charge of the workers to call them in and give them their money. He also told the man to begin with the ones who were hired last.
text: “Jãanʊɔr na wa ta, a puo sʊɔ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kaara a wʊ puo, ‘Bʊɔl a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ kʊ ba a ba libie, de nie piel a bala na bɩɛr wa tɩ tãn a bala na dɔ̃ɔ nie wa.’
translated: Even someone who is not a thief still fears the thief's eyes, because even the innocent are watched, so the thief's gaze is also upon them.

ref: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
text: “Vaa ir 'mɔɔ a bie nɩ a wʊ ma na fʊ chiin a Isɩral wɛr pɔ, a bala na dɔ̃ɔ bʊɔrɔ a bie kʊfʊ kpi naa.”
translated: They say that in the land of Israel, where they chose to dwell, the people are taking their own belongings and doing their own will.

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sʊɔr na nyɛ naa, ka a 'lɔ mɩ na yɔbɔ nyɛ, ka a pãn mɩ yuo kʊ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kpoor.
translated: They know the one who sees them, because he comes with them to the place, and he takes them there where they know the one in the forest.

ref: Many prophets and good people were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear. Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Mark
text: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ yɔɔ nɩ nɩtorsɩ yɔɔ ta bʊɔrɔ na ɩka ba nyɛ bon 'lɔ yɩn nyɛ tɩchɛ baa nyɛ ɛ, na ba ta wõ bon 'lɔ yɩn wõ tɩchɛ baa tʊ̃ɔ wõ ɛ.

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.

translated: If he goes to take it away, they will know that he took it away, and if he goes to take it properly, they will give him food to eat so that he may eat.

ref: So the chief priests met with the leaders and decided to bribe the soldiers with a lot of money.

ref: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
text: “Vaa ir 'mɔɔ a bie nɩ a wʊ ma na fʊ chiin a Isɩral wɛr pɔ, a bala na dɔ̃ɔ bʊɔrɔ a bie kʊfʊ kpi naa.”
translated: They say that in the land of Israel, where they chose to dwell, the people are taking their own belongings and doing their own will.

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sʊɔr na nyɛ naa, ka a 'lɔ mɩ na yɔbɔ nyɛ, ka a pãn mɩ yuo kʊ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kpoor.
translated: They know the one who sees them, because he comes with them to the place, and he takes them there where they know the one in the forest.

ref: Many prophets and good people were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear. Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Mark
text: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ yɔɔ nɩ nɩtorsɩ yɔɔ ta bʊɔrɔ na ɩka ba nyɛ bon 'lɔ yɩn nyɛ tɩchɛ baa nyɛ ɛ, na ba ta wõ bon 'lɔ yɩn wõ tɩchɛ baa tʊ̃ɔ wõ ɛ.
translated: Therefore, when you see the kʊrɔ there, there is Nãaŋmɩn's yɛ'yɛrbɛ; go and do the work so that they may have good and protect themselves, and so that you may have good and take care of yourselves properly.

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na tara yɔɔ, ban kʊ wʊn yɔɔ pʊɔ ka ba tara yɔɔ tɩchɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ba tara bon jaa ɛ, a bãlãa 'lɔ wʊn tara gba ban faan yin a wʊ sɛ̃.
translated: If he goes to take it away, they will know that he took it away, and if he goes to take it properly, they will give him food to eat so that he may eat.

ref: So the chief priests met with the leaders and decided to bribe the soldiers with a lot of money.
text: A bɔɔrlo nãa nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛrɛ na wa lɔ̃ɔ taa, ba guor taa de libiyɔɔ kʊ a sojasɩ
translated: This child is here to see so that we may go, but to go to the forest to find the path.

ref: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
text: “Vaa ir 'mɔɔ a bie nɩ a wʊ ma na fʊ chiin a Isɩral wɛr pɔ, a bala na dɔ̃ɔ bʊɔrɔ a bie kʊfʊ kpi naa.”
translated: Go, the one who is in the mother's house and does not know the Israel people, take the forest path to come and see now.

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sʊɔr na nyɛ naa, ka a 'lɔ mɩ na yɔbɔ nyɛ, ka a pãn mɩ yuo kʊ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kpoor.
translated: The matter is here, but he is with me here, and he brings me to Yuokʊ where the matter is at Kpoor.

ref: Many prophets and good people were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear. Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Mark

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na tara yɔɔ, ban kʊ wʊn yɔɔ pʊɔ ka ba tara yɔɔ tɩchɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ba tara bon jaa ɛ, a bãlãa 'lɔ wʊn tara gba ban faan yin a wʊ sɛ̃.
translated: When he comes to go there, they will stay there until he comes again, so they decided to go there to fetch it for him.

ref: So the chief priests met with the leaders and decided to bribe the soldiers with a lot of money.
text: A bɔɔrlo nãa nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛrɛ na wa lɔ̃ɔ taa, ba guor taa de libiyɔɔ kʊ a sojasɩ
translated: Here is the path where he comes and arrives, and goes to the guor and Libiyɔɔ to seek...

ref: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
text: “Vaa ir 'mɔɔ a bie nɩ a wʊ ma na fʊ chiin a Isɩral wɛr pɔ, a bala na dɔ̃ɔ bʊɔrɔ a bie kʊfʊ kpi naa.”
translated: They say that the land of Israel is where they live, the people of Dɔ̃ɔ Bʊɔrɔ who are known as kʊfʊ kpi naa.

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sʊɔr na nyɛ naa, ka a 'lɔ mɩ na yɔbɔ nyɛ, ka a pãn mɩ yuo kʊ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kpoor.
translated: There is a story about the sun and the people, but the story says that the people see, and the pãn says that they know there is a story about the moon.

ref: Many prophets and good people were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear. Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Mark
text: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ yɔɔ nɩ nɩtorsɩ yɔɔ ta bʊɔrɔ na ɩka ba nyɛ bon 'lɔ yɩn nyɛ tɩchɛ baa nyɛ ɛ, na ba ta wõ bon 'lɔ yɩn wõ tɩchɛ baa tʊ̃ɔ wõ ɛ.
translated: The elders say that when you go into the forest, you must follow the path of the Nãaŋmɩn people, which is the correct way, and if you take that path, you will find good and avoid harm, but if you take the other path, you will find evil and face danger.

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na tara yɔɔ, ban kʊ wʊn yɔɔ pʊɔ ka ba tara yɔɔ tɩchɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ba tara bon jaa ɛ, a bãlãa 'lɔ wʊn tara gba ban faan yin a wʊ sɛ̃.
translated: When he comes to go there, since they know that they will go there to do it, then when he comes there, they will send him along the path so that they see.

ref: So the chief priests met with the leaders and decided to bribe the soldiers with a lot of money.
text: A bɔɔrlo nãa nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛrɛ na wa lɔ̃ɔ taa, ba guor taa de libiyɔɔ kʊ a sojasɩ
translated: The old man went to the river to fetch water, and they went to Libya for work.

ref: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
text: “Vaa ir 'mɔɔ a bie nɩ a wʊ ma na fʊ chiin a Isɩral wɛr pɔ, a bala na dɔ̃ɔ bʊɔrɔ a bie kʊfʊ kpi naa.”
translated: There was a king in Israel who chose to do something in those days, and he brought to the people a certain custom that they practiced.

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sʊɔr na nyɛ naa, ka a 'lɔ mɩ na yɔbɔ nyɛ, ka a pãn mɩ yuo kʊ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kpoor.
translated: There is the sun and the day, because I go to that place, and I take my work there.

ref: Many prophets and good people were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear. Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Mark

translated: This thing is in the house and in the courtyard, and he took it to the village, and he kept it so that this thing remains in the storehouse.
